subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – The Proposal
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

Passages.paragraph: Lomov, a nervous landowner, visits his neighbour Chubukov’s house to propose marriage to Chubukov’s daughter, Natalya. He is anxious and careful about how he presents himself.
questions: Who is Lomov and why does he visit Chubukov’s house?
What is Lomov’s purpose in visiting?
How is Lomov feeling?
Which words show his nervousness?
Why is this paragraph important for setting the story?

Passages.paragraph: Natalya is outspoken and proud. She engages in arguments with Lomov about trivial matters, which escalates tension between them.
questions: How is Natalya described in this paragraph?
What does she argue about with Lomov?
Which words show her pride and assertiveness?
How does the argument affect the atmosphere?
Why is this paragraph important for the plot?

Passages.paragraph: Despite the arguments, Lomov continues with his marriage proposal. He is determined to win Natalya’s approval and is willing to overlook minor disagreements.
questions: What does Lomov continue to do despite the arguments?
Which words show his determination?
How does he handle minor disagreements?
What does this tell you about Lomov’s character?
Why is this paragraph important for the story’s development?

Passages.paragraph: Chubukov, observing the interaction, becomes anxious and intervenes occasionally to prevent the arguments from escalating. He is concerned about the outcome of the proposal.
questions: How does Chubukov react to Lomov and Natalya’s arguments?
What actions does he take to manage the situation?
Which words show his concern?
Why is Chubukov’s intervention important?
What is the main idea of this paragraph?

Passages.paragraph: Eventually, after many misunderstandings and quarrels, Lomov and Natalya reconcile. The proposal is accepted, and the story humorously portrays human pride, stubbornness, and the complexities of relationships.
questions: What happens at the end of the proposal?
How do Lomov and Natalya resolve their quarrels?
Which words show the humorous aspect of the story?
What human traits are highlighted in this paragraph?
What is the main idea of this paragraph?
